_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how to use colons and brackets to support and extend meaning in our writing.

_source.w: We are learning how to use colons and brackets to support and extend meaning in our writing.
y: Colons and brackets are sophisticated punctuation tools that help us organise and elaborate ideas.
Using these correctly adds clarity and polish to our academic and creative writing.
Understanding these conventions helps us read complex texts more fluently.
eg: We use colons to introduce lists, explanations or examples — 'There are three reasons: cost, time and quality.'
We use brackets to add supplementary information — 'The novel (first published in 1988) won several awards.'
We find colons and brackets in published texts and explain their function.
We practise incorporating colons and brackets naturally into our own writing.
We discuss when to use brackets versus commas for parenthetical information.
We edit a text to add colons and brackets where they would improve clarity.
